Now we're going to practice. Let us pretend for a moment that your boss comes up to you at three o'clock in the afternoon and asks you to finish report by the end of the day. You're frustrated because you know for a fact that this report will take at least four hours to complete and you have to leave by 5 p.m. to pick up your child from practice. In the past any attempt at asking for a deadline extension no matter what the reason has been perceived by your boss is a lack of dedication to your job and lazy. So your goal is to get your boss to push back your deadline so that you can leave on time to pick up your child while avoiding the perception that you're lazy. Your boss pops his head into your office and says I need the quarterly report on my desk by the end of the day. How should you respond?
